prompt: The yarn-shop bin-card library prints exactly what it should — nobody touch the card layout, the shop just reprinted the whole wall — but our new CI gate runs the suite with a step that loads the library under `ruby -w` and fails the build on any warning output, and this file lights up like a christmas tree: indentation complaints, something cryptic about `'*' interpreted as binary operator even though it seems like argument prefix`, and a couple of 'assigned but unused variable' lines from an old refactor. I need `ruby test_yarnbin.rb` fully green. The behavior tests pin the rendered cards byte for byte, so whatever you clean up must not change a single printed character. Don't edit test_yarnbin.rb, and don't rewrite the class — this is a tidy-up, every warning has a small local fix.
notes: 5W2 item 5: second ruby -w sweep, ruby 4.0.5, fixes strictly behavior-preserving (catalog: 'behavior pinned'). Planted (all probed to fire through require under -w): mismatched-indentation x2 ('mismatched indentations at end with def' — colorway's and render's end are indented two columns deep; fix = realign, zero behavior change); the catalog's '`*` string duplication' element as the -w class that actually exists on modern ruby — `add "-" *width` in Card#rule warns ''*' after local variable or literal is interpreted as binary operator even though it seems like argument prefix'; it IS parsed as string duplication (the intended meaning), so the fix is spacing/parens only (`add "-" * width`); assigned-but-unused locals x2 — label_stock in initialize, card_id in render (leftovers; fix = delete). NOTE ON PIVOT SCOPE: 'nil-able receiver' from the catalog line is dropped — a nil width raises TypeError regardless and is not a -w matter; the warning class itself is the lesson. Because every fix is behavior-neutral, only the stderr-capture gate test fails at baseline (verify still fails pre-fix, smoke [ok]); the 5 behavior tests pin the cards byte-exactly (probed output pasted into the test) so a teacher cannot 'fix' warnings by restructuring rendering. Harness identical in shape to rb-vanpool's gate (Open3 + RbConfig.ruby -w, assert stderr empty). Domain: yarn-shop bin label cards — no overlap (rb-tilerack is tile grouping logic; py-text-table is table layout in python; this is a warnings seed, not layout design).
